articles.title: Joey Barton Arrested After Fight
articles.summary: Joey Barton, a former football player, was arrested after a fight near a golf club. It happened on Sunday night in Fairway, Merseyside. Police found a man with injuries to his face and ribs. Two men, including Barton, were arrested for hurting someone. The injured man went to the hospital. Barton used to play for teams like Manchester City and Newcastle. He stopped playing in 2017 and became a manager. This news is from the BBC, a trusted source.

articles.title: A Big Fire Closes Glasgow Central Station
articles.summary: Glasgow Central Station, a very busy train station in Scotland, is closed because of a big fire. The fire started in a vape shop next door and spread to an old building. The building is now falling down, so it is not safe. The station has been shut since Sunday, and trains cannot stop there. People need to use other stations like Queen Street or check the ScotRail website for updates. Firefighters and workers are still at the site to make sure everything is safe. The station might be closed for a few more days, so passengers should be patient. Luckily, the station itself is not badly damaged. This is a big problem because many people use this station every year.

articles.title: Olivia Dean Stars at Big Music Festival
articles.summary: Olivia Dean is a famous singer who will be the main act on the last day of a big music festival called Radio 1's Big Weekend. The festival happens in Sunderland at Herrington Country Park from May 22 to 24. On Sunday, Olivia Dean will perform with other singers like Niall Horan and Kehlani. On Saturday, singers like Zara Larsson and Louis Tomlinson will play, and on Friday, DJs Fatboy Slim and Sonny Fodera will start the festival. Olivia Dean recently won many awards, including at the Brits and a Grammy, making her very popular. This is her first time headlining a big UK show. Other artists like CMAT and Myles Smith will also sing on Sunday, and there are more performers on different stages. The festival is a fun event with lots of music for everyone to enjoy.

articles.title: Arizona Election Records Given to FBI
articles.summary: In Arizona, a state leader named Warren Petersen gave records about the 2020 election to the FBI. This happened because the Trump administration is investigating the election, even though many checks found no big problems. The FBI got similar records from Georgia earlier this year. Arizona's Attorney General Kris Mayes said this investigation is based on false stories and is not a real law enforcement effort. In 2021, a company hired by Republicans looked for fraud but found nothing important and used strange ideas like checking for bamboo on ballots. This shows that some people are still questioning the election results without good evidence.

articles.title: Social Media Scare: How Parents and Kids Reacted to School Fight Rumors
articles.summary: In February, some schools in London had a scary day because of social media posts. The posts talked about 'red vs blue' fights between students from different schools. Teachers and police were worried, so they stood outside schools to keep kids safe after class. But no real fights happened. It turned out that many kids didn't even see the posts first—their parents did! Parents shared the posts in group chats, which made everyone more worried. Social media companies like TikTok and Snapchat removed a few posts, but they said not many kids were searching for this until parents talked about it. This shows that sometimes parents and kids see social media differently, and fear can spread quickly even when there's no real danger.

articles.title: A Soldier from Kentucky Dies in a War
articles.summary: A soldier named Benjamin Pennington from Kentucky died after being hurt in an attack in Saudi Arabia. He was 26 years old and joined the Army after high school. The Pentagon said he is the seventh U.S. soldier to die in the Iran war. He was wounded on March 1 at an air base and passed away on Sunday. People who knew him, like a pastor and a local leader, said he was quiet and hardworking. He was part of a unit that works with satellites and missiles. The governor of Kentucky called him a hero for serving the country. This sad event has affected his family and community in Glendale, a small town in Kentucky.
